metadata.title: SDNY/DOJ crypto-privacy prosecution practices — Samourai, Roman Storm/Tornado Cash, Pertsev, and the broader money-transmitter/developer-liability pattern (NEUTRAL, graded)
metadata.frame: A deliberately NEUTRAL map of how SDNY and DOJ have prosecuted crypto-PRIVACY software — presenting BOTH the prosecution's rationale (real laundering, incl. DPRK; the unlicensed-money-transmitter statute) AND the defense/critique (FinCEN's own view that non-custodial software isn't a money transmitter; an alleged Brady non-disclosure; a DOJ policy memo rejecting this model that SDNY proceeded against anyway; the 'criminalizing neutral code' / developer-liability chilling effect; a jury that hung on the serious counts). The point is the PATTERN and its tensions, graded — not a 'corruption' verdict.
metadata.WARNING: Grade + neutrality discipline. The case facts (charges, the FinCEN Aug-2023 call, the Brady motion + its denial, the Blanche memo, the verdicts/sentences, Pertsev's Dutch conviction) are DOCUMENTED. 'Malicious prosecution / corruption' is an investigative framing — recorded as a question, NOT asserted as a finding. The COUNTERWEIGHT (Tornado Cash laundered $1B+ incl. for Lazarus/DPRK; real victim harm) is given equal billing. Both sides graded; no verdict on intent. Overlay edges excluded from the proofs.
metadata.method: DOJ/SDNY + IRS-CI press; CoinDesk/Bitcoin Magazine/crypto.news (the FinCEN call + Brady motion); Mayer Brown/Nat-Law-Review (verdict analysis); Dutch court reporting (Pertsev); Coin Center (the code/privacy critique); FinCEN 2019 guidance. Each cell graded; prosecution and defense positions both stated.

samourai_wallet.case: US v. Rodriguez & Hill (SDNY) — Keonne Rodriguez & William Lonergan Hill, founders of the non-custodial Bitcoin mixing wallet Samourai; charged Apr 2024 with conspiracy to commit money laundering + conspiracy to operate an unlicensed money-transmitting business.
samourai_wallet.prosecution_view: Samourai's Whirlpool/Ricochet features were used to launder proceeds (DOJ alleged >$100M of criminal proceeds, incl. dark-market/fraud funds); operating as an unlicensed money transmitter.

defense_and_documented_tensions.detail: On an Aug 23 2023 call, FinCEN personnel told prosecutors they did NOT believe Samourai was a 'money transmitting business' because the wallet is NON-CUSTODIAL (consistent with FinCEN's 2019 guidance that anonymizing-software providers / non-custodial wallets are generally not money transmitters). Charges were filed ~6 months later anyway.
The defense argued DOJ withheld that exculpatory FinCEN call for ~14 months; moved to dismiss (May 2025). The motion was DENIED without a written opinion.

In Apr 2025 DOJ (Deputy AG Todd Blanche) disbanded the National Cryptocurrency Enforcement Team and directed prosecutors to STOP targeting mixing/tumbling services and non-custodial wallets for users' conduct. Samourai prosecutors sought a 16-day extension to consider dropping — but did NOT drop the case.

samourai_wallet.outcome: Rodriguez and Hill pleaded guilty and were sentenced to 5 and 4 years (Judge Denise Cote, Nov 2025) — i.e., the prosecution proceeded to conviction despite FinCEN's stated view AND the DOJ policy memo rejecting this model.
samourai_wallet.grade: fact (charges, FinCEN call, Brady denial, Blanche memo, sentences); 'malicious prosecution' is an investigative framing, not asserted

roman_storm_tornado_cash.case: US v. Roman Storm (SDNY) — Tornado Cash co-developer; tried 2025.
roman_storm_tornado_cash.verdict: Aug 6 2025: jury CONVICTED Storm on conspiracy to operate an unlicensed money-transmitting business, but HUNG (after Allen charges, ~4 days) on the more serious counts — conspiracy to commit money laundering and conspiracy to violate sanctions. Mar 9 2026: DOJ requested a RETRIAL on the deadlocked counts (proposed Oct 2026).
roman_storm_tornado_cash.prosecution_view: Tornado Cash was used to launder >$1B incl. funds from the DPRK/Lazarus Ronin hack; Storm knowingly operated/profited from an unlicensed transmitting business.

defense_and_critique.detail: Tornado Cash smart contracts are immutable and non-custodial — the developers don't hold user funds; the defense/Coin Center argue prosecuting this is 'criminalizing the publication of software code' and neutral privacy tooling.
defense_and_critique.grade: fact (the legal argument); developer-liability question UNSETTLED

defense_and_critique.detail: SDNY argued the FinCEN/Samourai opinion was 'irrelevant' to the Storm case — a contested legal position the defense rejected.
The jury's inability to convict on money-laundering/sanctions conspiracy is itself a data point on the theory's strength.

roman_storm_tornado_cash.grade: fact (split verdict + retrial request); the developer-liability precedent is genuinely unsettled

pertsev_netherlands.detail: Tornado Cash co-developer Alexey Pertsev was convicted in the Netherlands (May 2024), sentenced to 64 months for facilitating ~$1.2B of laundering (Jul 2019–Aug 2022); a judge characterized Tornado Cash as primarily a criminal tool. Released pending appeal (electronic monitor, 2025).

broader_pattern.title: Beyond the three cases — the pattern and its tensions

points.item: The money-transmitter theory on non-custodial software
points.detail: The core legal move is applying the unlicensed-money-transmitter statute to software whose developers never custody funds — in tension with FinCEN's 2019 guidance. Whether that theory is valid is the central, unsettled question across Samourai/Storm.

points.item: The OFAC Tornado Cash sanction (separate track)
points.detail: OFAC sanctioned the Tornado Cash smart contracts (Aug 2022 — first sanctioning of immutable code); the 5th Circuit VACATED it (Nov 2024) and Treasury lifted it (Mar 2025) — a court rejecting the parallel sanctions theory even as the criminal cases proceeded (spec-onchain-threat-actor-addresses).

The prosecutions coincided with privacy-wallet/mixer operators (e.g., Wasabi/zkSNACKs, Phoenix) restricting or exiting the US — a documented chilling effect on lawful-privacy software, regardless of intent.

Critics note incumbent banks were permitted to run dollar tokens (JPM Coin) while crypto-native privacy devs faced criminal charges (cf the Ripple-vs-JPM-Coin asymmetry, spec-crypto-banking-debanking) — a selective-enforcement CONCERN, graded, not a proven claim.

The 2025 deregulatory posture (Blanche memo, OFAC vacatur, the SEC/CFTC crypto reset) sits awkwardly beside SDNY continuing Samourai to a guilty plea — the executive saying 'stop' while a US Attorney's office proceeded.

the_counterweight: For neutrality: the harm was real. Tornado Cash processed >$1B of illicit flows including the DPRK/Lazarus Ronin proceeds; Samourai mixed criminal proceeds; both Pertsev (Dutch) and Storm (one count) were convicted by juries/courts. The defensible prosecution interest is real money-laundering and sanctions evasion — the dispute is whether the money-transmitter/developer-liability THEORY is the lawful way to reach it, and whether the conduct of these specific prosecutions (the FinCEN non-disclosure, proceeding against the policy memo) was proper. Both sides are stated; neither 'corruption' nor 'pure persecution' is asserted as a finding.

edges.raw_instrument: money-laundering + unlicensed-money-transmitter prosecution (2024); 5/4-yr sentences (2025) despite FinCEN's non-custodial view + the Blanche memo
edges.note: OVERLAY: proceeded against agency guidance + DOJ policy

edges.raw_instrument: US v. Roman Storm: convicted unlicensed-money-transmitting; HUNG on laundering/sanctions; retrial sought (2026)
Aug-2023 call: FinCEN viewed non-custodial Samourai as NOT a money transmitter (2019-guidance-consistent); disclosure delayed ~14mo (Brady motion, denied)
the counterweight: DPRK laundered Ronin/hack proceeds through the mixer (>$1B total illicit)

limits: DOCUMENTED: the charges, the Aug-2023 FinCEN call + the denied Brady motion, the Blanche memo, the Samourai 5/4-yr sentences, the Storm split verdict + retrial request, Pertsev's Dutch conviction, the OFAC vacatur, the privacy-tool exits. NEUTRALITY: the prosecution interest (real DPRK/laundering harm; jury/court convictions) is given equal weight to the defense critique (FinCEN's own view; Brady; criminalizing non-custodial code; proceeding against the policy memo). NOT asserted: that the prosecutions were 'corrupt' or 'malicious' (an investigative framing, recorded as the open question) — nor that they were clearly proper. The developer-liability/money-transmitter THEORY is genuinely unsettled law. Overlay edges excluded from the proofs.
